questions.question: What is the most basic driver behind the seasonal reversal associated with the Indian monsoon?
options: Only volcanic activity in the Indian Ocean
Differential heating of land and sea creating seasonal pressure changes
Permanent high pressure over the Himalayas throughout the year
Rotation of the Earth without any role for pressure gradients
questions.explanation: The land-sea heating contrast creates the seasonal pressure pattern that underlies the monsoon system.

questions.question: Why do the Western Ghats receive heavy rainfall during the southwest monsoon?
options: Because they lie in a major desert belt
Because they block retreating winter winds only
Because moist winds are forced to rise over the relief, producing orographic rainfall
Because they are far from the Arabian Sea
questions.explanation: Moist monsoon winds are forced upward by the Western Ghats, cool, and condense into heavy rainfall.

questions.question: Which region is especially known for receiving important rainfall during the retreating monsoon season?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu benefits significantly from retreating monsoon rainfall, unlike many other parts of India that receive most of their rain earlier.
